title: Desktop App Runtime Performance Audit
consumer_purpose: Identify and prioritize performance improvements for the desktop app — startup time, main process initialization, sidecar boot, renderer loading, IPC overhead, bundle size, and dependency bloat
claim_atoms: Startup sequence mapped with phase timings and blocking operations identified
Port discovery dance eliminated — sidecar reports its own port
IPC handler registration made lazy — only essential handlers registered early
Sidecar health check made non-blocking — main window shown before sidecar ready
electron-context-menu deferred to after window creation
CA certificate loading made async, not blocking early startup phases
Bundle optimization targets identified with estimated savings
Lazy-loading opportunities for marked, @zip.js/zip.js, electron-store catalogued
Renderer CSS bundle and i18n lazy-loading assessed
content:
Desktop App Runtime Performance Audit

== SCOPE ==
Audit the Electron desktop application at packages/desktop/ for runtime performance, covering main process startup, sidecar boot, renderer loading, build/bundle optimization, and dependency weight.

== FINDINGS SUMMARY ==

1. Startup Sequence (Main Process)
The main entry at packages/desktop/src/main/index.ts has 22 sequential phases:

Phase A — Module-level imports (before any function runs):
- electron-context-menu imported eagerly at module level
- Effect framework imported
- @opencode-ai/app/desktop-menu imported in ipc.ts
- All main process modules loaded at require time

Phase B — Synchronous startup within main Effect generator:
1. contextMenu() — sync call to electron-context-menu, ~5-15ms
2. process.chdir(homedir()) — ~1ms
3. 5 env vars set — ~1ms
4. App identity setup (paths, names) — ~5ms
5. initLogging() — file I/O, creates directories, ~20-50ms
6. initCrashReporter() — ~10ms
7. CA certificate loading via getCACertificates() — can be 100-500ms
8. Proxy env setup — ~5ms
9. Single instance lock check — ~1ms
10. preferAppEnv() — reads .env file, ~10ms
11. Event handler registration — ~2ms
12. registerIpcHandlers() — registers 50+ IPC handlers in bulk, ~20-50ms

Phase C — After app.whenReady():
13. migrate() — file system checks for old Tauri data, ~10-50ms
14. Protocol registration, dock icon, auto-updater setup — ~20ms
15. startNetLog() — ~10ms
16. Port discovery — creates TCP server on port 0, listens, then closes it. ~50-100ms. Unnecessary overhead.
17. Sidecar spawn via utilityProcess.fork() + health check polling up to 30 seconds
18. Conditional loading window creation (only if sidecar not ready in 1s)
19. Await loading task
20. Create main window
21. Create menu

2. Critical Performance Issues

ISSUE 1 (HIGH): Port Discovery Dance — The main process creates a TCP server just to find a free port, then closes it and passes the port to the sidecar. The sidecar could simply listen on port 0 and report its bound port back via its ready message. Saves ~50-100ms.

ISSUE 2 (HIGH): Sidecar Health Check Blocks Main Window — After forking the sidecar, the main process waits for 'ready' signal then polls GET /global/health with exponential backoff up to 30 seconds. The main window is only shown after this succeeds. This is the single biggest startup delay.

ISSUE 3 (HIGH): All IPC Handlers Registered Eagerly — 50+ IPC handlers in a single registerIpcHandlers() call before app.whenReady(). Many are rarely used (store operations, updater, WSL config).

ISSUE 4 (MEDIUM): electron-context-menu Loaded Early — Imported at module level and called in first line of the main Effect. Adds ~15-50ms. Could be deferred to after window creation.

ISSUE 5 (MEDIUM): CA Certificate Loading — getCACertificates('default') + getCACertificates('system') can take 100-500ms on some systems. Blocks the entire startup queue.

ISSUE 6 (MEDIUM): No Code Splitting in Main Bundle — electron-vite bundles everything into a single chunk (only node-pty externalized). effect, drizzle-orm, electron-store, marked, @zip.js/zip.js all loaded eagerly despite some being rarely used.

3. Dependency Weight Analysis

Main process runtime deps by estimated size and usage pattern:
- electron: ~130MB binary, unavoidable
- effect: ~1MB JS, used for sidecar orchestration
- electron-log: ~200KB, for logging
- electron-store: ~100KB, config persistence
- electron-context-menu: ~50KB, right-click menu
- electron-updater: ~300KB, auto-update
- electron-window-state: ~20KB, window position
- marked: ~200KB, markdown parsing (could be deferred)
- @zip.js/zip.js: ~500KB, debug log export (rarely used)
- drizzle-orm: ~1MB, DB ORM (only in sidecar process)
- @opencode-ai/app: workspace, UI framework
- @opencode-ai/ui: workspace, theme/components

4. Build Configuration
- electron-vite bundles main into single chunk with only node-pty externalized
- WASM files (tree-sitter, DuckDB) copied as assets to out/main/chunks/
- Sidecar is a separate entry point (src/main/sidecar.ts)
- Renderer has two entry points: main app + loading screen
- Sentry source maps on renderer

== RECOMMENDED IMPROVEMENT SLICES ==

Slice 1 — Immediate Wins (Low Risk, High Impact):
1. Eliminate port discovery: sidecar listens on port 0, reports port via ready message
2. Defer electron-context-menu: move to after window creation
3. Defer IPC handler registration: register only essential handlers early, lazy-register rest
4. Move CA certificate loading: make async/non-blocking

Slice 2 — Sidecar Bootstrap (Medium Impact):
5. Non-blocking sidecar health check: show main window immediately, show 'connecting' state
6. Parallel loading: trigger sidecar fork earlier in the startup sequence

Slice 3 — Bundle Optimization (Medium Impact):
7. Code-split main process: dynamic import for @zip.js/zip.js, marked, electron-store
8. Analyze effect import surface: only import what's needed for sidecar flow

Slice 4 — Renderer Optimization (Lower Priority):
9. CSS bundle audit: Tailwind output likely oversized
10. Lazy locale loading: only load the current locale
11. Tree-shake unused Solid components
